insights.title: LARGE APARTMENTS OUTPERFORMS AT 6.8%
insights.finding: The Large Apartments campaign generates 6.8% quality on 88 leads — more than double the Bungalows campaign (3.1% on 644 leads) and 4× the Nehru Nagar campaign (0.9% on 106 leads), yet receives a fraction of the lead volume and presumably budget allocation.
insights.action: Reallocate at least 25% of Bungalows campaign budget to Large Apartments. The quality signal is statistically meaningful at 88 leads — this is not noise.

insights.title: DEAD CAMPAIGNS BURNING BUDGET NOW
insights.finding: Four campaigns — Nim Sg222 (86 leads), Godrej (62 leads), MASTER New Leads (23 leads), and GENERAL AHMD AD (8 leads) — collectively account for 179 leads and a combined quality rate of exactly 0%, meaning zero qualified leads produced from this entire spend.
insights.action: Kill all four campaigns immediately and redirect their budgets to Large Apartments and Bungalows. Zero quality across 179 leads is not a cold-start problem — it is a structural targeting or creative misalignment.

insights.title: DOCTORS ARE YOUR STEALTH SEGMENT
insights.finding: Medical/Healthcare professionals represent only 28 leads but convert at 3.6% quality — the highest of any named profession — yet the ₹7–10Cr Ahmedabad Medical profile specifically converts at 16.7% on 6 leads, making it the second-highest converting profile in the entire CRM.
insights.action: Launch a dedicated Meta audience targeting medical professionals (doctors, surgeons, hospital owners) aged 35–55 in Ahmedabad with creatives and copy engineered for their psychology: privacy, prestige, time-efficiency, and asset security. Do not mix this audience with entrepreneur creatives.

insights.title: FUNNEL IS BLEEDING AT CONVERSATION STAGE
insights.finding: Of 1,023 received leads, only 125 (12.2%) have been spoken to, 27 are exploring, and zero — literally 0 — have reached HWC or Hot stage, meaning the entire pipeline has stalled before any deal-stage conversation has begun.
insights.action: This is not a lead quality problem — it is a sales process failure. Immediately audit why 898 leads were never contacted and implement a 24-hour mandatory first-touch SLA with a dedicated inside sales team. The funnel is not leaking; it has no floor.

insights.title: 10CR+ SEGMENT UNDERPERFORMS ITS PROMISE
insights.finding: The ₹10Cr+ segment has 168 leads but converts at only 1.8% quality — lower than the ₹5–7Cr band (4.2%) and nearly identical to the sub-₹5Cr band (1.7%), suggesting ultra-HNI leads are either aspirational browsers or the product portfolio does not have a compelling enough ₹10Cr+ offering to close them.
insights.action: Either create an exclusive, curated ₹10Cr+ product experience (private viewings, architecture-first storytelling, bespoke concierge) that matches the expectation of a genuine ultra-HNI buyer, or stop advertising to this segment until that product reality exists. Advertising luxury at a tier you cannot deliver on destroys brand credibility.

insights.title: REAL ESTATE PROFESSIONALS ARE NOISE, NOT BUYERS
insights.finding: Real Estate professionals account for 25 leads with a 0% quality rate — every single lead from this segment has failed to convert — strongly suggesting this audience is competitor research or professional curiosity, not genuine buyer intent.
insights.action: Exclude Real Estate as a profession from all Meta targeting immediately. Add a lead qualification question in the ad form or landing page that screens out agents and brokers. Every rupee spent on this segment is funding competitor intelligence.

insights.title: DELHI PROFILE SIGNALS OUTBOUND NRI PLAY
insights.finding: The Other/₹7–10Cr/New Delhi profile converts at 33.3% — the single highest quality rate in the entire CRM on 3 leads — which, while a small sample, hints that out-of-market HNIs buying Ahmedabad real estate as an investment or relocation asset are disproportionately serious buyers.
insights.action: Build a dedicated channel for non-Ahmedabad HNIs: target NRI communities in the UAE, UK, and US alongside Delhi/Mumbai buyers using investment-framing messaging ('Ahmedabad's decade of infrastructure: why smart capital is moving here now'). This is a separate buyer psychology requiring a separate funnel.

insights.title: MAY SPIKE WAS ARTIFICIAL VOLUME, NOT GROWTH
insights.finding: May 2026 generated 454 leads — the peak month — followed by a -50.2% crash, yet quality rates across the dataset remain uniformly low, suggesting the May campaign drove high-volume, low-intent traffic rather than a genuine demand surge that then corrected.
insights.action: Reframe your campaign KPI from cost-per-lead to cost-per-qualified-lead immediately. Brief your media agency that a month producing 50 high-quality leads at ₹5,000 CPL is worth more than 450 junk leads at ₹500 CPL. Restructure performance incentives accordingly.
